title: Calverton Fairey Battle L5499 Memorial
cause_of_loss: Lost in fog and blackout conditions returning from France; German intruder kept Swinderby blacked out
narrative: The earliest crash in the Farnsfield area and the only one involving a Fairey Battle — a light bomber already obsolete by 1940. 300 Squadron was Poland's first bomber squadron in exile, formed from Polish airmen who had escaped occupied Poland to continue fighting from Britain. Returning from bombing Calais harbour, fog rolled across Nottinghamshire. A German intruder kept RAF Swinderby blacked out, forcing homing bombers to wait until fuel was nearly exhausted. F/O Gebicki, an experienced 39-year-old pilot, attempted to land in darkness and crashed at Watchwood Plantation. The memorial — a steel cross with the inscription 'Perished for our and your freedom' — was erected by coal miners building the railway to Calverton Colliery. In 2019, Gebicki's son, Professor Janusz Gebicki, flew from Australia to attend a memorial service.

crew.name: Jan Gebicki
crew.fate_detail: Killed when aircraft crashed in fog and darkness. His son Professor Janusz Gebicki flew from Australia in 2019 for memorial service.
crew.bio: Experienced 39-year-old Polish pilot who escaped occupied Poland.

crew.name: Edward Morawa
crew.fate_detail: Killed in the crash. Buried at Wilford Hill Cemetery.

inscription: Perished for our and your freedom
erected_by: Coal miners building Calverton Colliery railway

external_narratives.summary: Records crash of Fairey Battle Mk.I L5499/BH-Y of 300 (Mazowiecki) Squadron on 13 October 1940.
White stone pillar with two plaques and tubular steel cross.
key_details: White stone pillar with two plaques and tubular steel cross
